title: australopyrum pectinatum subsp. retrofractum
intro: Australopyrum pectinatum subsp. retrofractum, also known as the Australian bitter pea or Australian bitter vetch, is a species of plant in the legume family Fabaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and rheumatism due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Australopyrum pectinatum subsp. retrofractum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, improve circulation, and reduce pain and inflammation. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
